packageName: Belavados Deep Voice Math 24 JSON Pack
requestedChange: Complete deep overwrite of speech math using the uploaded repository resources while keeping Belavados races, gender identities, biomes, fantasy accent names, and real-world accent inspirations intact.

jsonFileRoles.01-22: One deep voice-math JSON per Belavados racial category.
jsonFileRoles.23: Classes and races deep voice-math merge file.

racialCategory.name: Celestial, Fiendish, and Planar Bloodlines
racialCategory.dominionInfluence: Mistress of crossroads, curses, planar mysteries, and supernatural bloodlines.
racialCategory.categorySpeechSummary: Otherworldly resonance; celestial lines brighten, fiendish lines harden, cursed lines roughen.
racialCategory.accentInteraction: Apply the biome accent first, then let this racial category reshape intelligibility, resonance, rhythm, consonants, and nonhuman textures.
racialCategory.biomeRoutingNote: Recommended biome accents are carriers; this racial category still applies its own resonance/articulation overlay after the biome is selected.

repositoryResourceUse.sourceUsePolicy: Repository files were scanned/extracted locally and used as design/math references. This JSON pack stores derived equations, parameter names, pipeline ideas, and calibration notes; it does not copy repository source code.

repositoriesIntegrated.primaryUse: Realtime orchestration, streaming-friendly voice state, session pipeline separation, and live agent/event flow concepts.
Reference-audio calibration corpus for short utterance timing, mono WAV consistency, and base-audio influence behavior.
Multi-voice packaging and Tacotron2/ParallelWaveGAN-style separation of acoustic model and vocoder.
Voice-pack packaging conventions and user-owned voice-resource separation.
TTS architecture references including VITS, duration prediction, mel spectrogram style references, speaker embeddings/d-vectors, and vocoder handoff.
Synthetic voice generation workflow and prompt-to-voice design framing.
Vocoder artifact awareness and mel-filterbank math for quality/safety checks.
Latent audio pipeline concepts, audio dataset/inference structure, and diffusion-style audio generation separation.
Browser SpeechSynthesis voice selection, utterance creation, rate, and pitch routing.
REST TTS request shaping, voice/audioFormat/sampleRate fields, and audio-output parameterization.
Browser voice inventory and utterancePitch/utteranceRate controls.
Unified TTS backend protocol, voice cloning/design/auto voice, audio resampling/trimming/fade utilities, longform parser, voice tags, pause tags, per-span speed.
Voice-name mapping, SSML rate conversion, and streaming TTS response handling.
Voice generation app behavior: timbre, language, playback speed, upload/recording, mp3 output, voice-to-voice, and cloning UX.

repositoryResourceUse.notCopiedCodeNote: No executable repository source code is embedded. Formulas and JSON control fields are clean, derived, implementation-ready abstractions for the user's static voice studio/scanner.

normalization.blendRule: final = baseAudio * influenceBaseAudio + synthesizedTransform * (1 - influenceBaseAudio), then biome/race/class/gender/personality/emotion weights are applied as independent lambdas.

userFacingSliders.name: Voice Height
userFacingSliders.controls: Base pitch, F0, semitone shift, pitch range

userFacingSliders.name: Hesitations
userFacingSliders.controls: Stutter chance, hesitation text, pause behavior

userFacingSliders.name: Gruff Edge
userFacingSliders.controls: Rough cadence, jitter/shimmer hints, gruff stress

influenceSliders.name: Race / Ancestry Influence
influenceSliders.controls: How strongly ancestry/species modifies the base voice

influenceSliders.name: Gender Identity Influence
influenceSliders.controls: How strongly gender-linked style affects the voice

influenceSliders.name: Accent Strength / Remove Accent
influenceSliders.controls: How strongly biome/accent rules appear or fade

influenceSliders.name: Uploaded / Recorded Voice Influence
influenceSliders.controls: How much the original clip remains in the result

influenceSliders.name: Emotion Strength
influenceSliders.controls: How strongly emotion curves reshape pitch, timing, and tone

primaryEngineParameters.pitchRangeSemitones: range = lerp(0.75, 10.0, inflection_n) * racePitchRangeScale * genderPitchRangeScale * emotionPitchRangeScale
primaryEngineParameters.speechRate: rate = clamp(0.45, 2.25, 2^(0.9 * speed_z) * raceRate * classRate * emotionRate)

analysisPipeline: Decode MP3/OGG/WAV to mono Float32 PCM; preserve original sample rate metadata.
Resample working copy to engine rate; reference rates observed in uploaded resources include 24k, 22.05k, 44.1k, and 48k.
Run VAD/silence trim; keep a silence map for pauseControl and stutter injection.
Extract STFT magnitude/phase; derive mel spectrogram for acoustic/vocoder conditioning.
Estimate F0 contour, voicing probability, phrase-level pitch range, and terminal cadence.
Estimate formants through LPC-style envelope tracking; map resonance/mouthShape/throatDepth to formant scaling.
Estimate breath/noise, harmonic-to-noise, jitter, shimmer, RMS, spectral tilt, and nasal-band energy.
Build baseVoiceVector, then blend biome accent, race/lineage, class/subclass, gender identity, personality, and emotion vectors.
Transform with PSOLA/vocoder/browser TTS/voice-to-voice path depending runtime engine capability.
Run artifact-aware QA using mel residual, clipping, DC offset, sibilance, robotic steadiness, and synthetic-vocoder artifact checks.

repositoryDerivedEquations.durationTransform: duration_i' = duration_i * durationScale * raceDurationScale * classDurationScale * genderDurationScale * emotionDurationScale * (1 + epsilon_i)
repositoryDerivedEquations.formantTransform: F_j' = F_j * formantScale + deltaMouth_j + deltaThroat_j + deltaNasal_j
repositoryDerivedEquations.vocoderConditioning: waveform = vocoder(mel, speakerVector, styleVector, durationVector, noiseSchedule)
repositoryDerivedEquations.artifactScore: artifactRisk = w1*melResidual + w2*phaseDiscontinuity + w3*overSmoothness + w4*unvoicedVoicedLeak + w5*clipping

runtimeMergeModel.compactFormula: V_final = blendAudio(V_baseAudio, Synthesize(V_neutral + λ_b*B_biome + λ_l*L_lineage + λ_r*R_race + λ_c*C_class + λ_s*S_subclass + λ_g*G_gender + λ_p*P_personality + λ_e*E_emotion + ε_human), influenceBaseAudio)

expandedEquations.baseAudio: features_base = AnalyzePCM(uploadedOrRecordedAudio); keep = influenceBaseAudio; finalFeatures = keep*features_base + (1-keep)*mergedFantasyFeatures
expandedEquations.finalFormants: F_j_final = F_j_base * Π formantScales + Σ formantDeltas_j
expandedEquations.finalWave: x_final = VocoderOrPSOLA(mel_final, f0_final, duration_final, styleVector_final) + textureLayers

safetyPermissionLayer.disallowedDefault: Do not train or clone from private, celebrity, commercial, or unknown-rights voices without permission.
safetyPermissionLayer.packBehavior: This JSON describes controllable fictional voice traits and accent-inspired mouth-feel; it does not contain voice-cloning weights or copied audio.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Humans sound closest to the base. Elves become breezier and more elegant, especially sea-traders or sky-dock elves. Dwarves and gnomes become harsher, shipwright-like, and practical. Aquatic peoples make it fluid and tide-shaped. Ocean Dril'thar use Tidecrest Cant when living in floating settlements, storm-pools, mangrove platforms, or ocean pilgrim ports. Fire, storm, and water Genasi shift the voice toward heat, thunder, or wave rhythm. Warforged and constructs sound like harbor machinery, bells, brass hulls, and pressure valves.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Merchants sound like dock traders; captains, paladins, fighters, and commanders sound declarative; bards sound sea-shanty-like; artificers sound shipyard-practical; clerics sound like tide-priests.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.finalVoiceFeel: Open, rolling, maritime, lively, salt-air social.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Sea Elves, reef elves, aquatic Kaluseban, merfolk, tritons, reefborn Dril'thar, and coral-adapted beastfolk should use this instead of their dryland family accent. Elves make it delicate and precise. Aquatic races make it especially fluid. Orcish or goblinoid aquatic lineages become abrupt and unusual. Reptilian reef lineages add sharp consonants and controlled throat pressure. Coral plantfolk sound chiming, slow, and breath-light.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Clerics sound ritualistic and tide-temple formal; mages sound precise; rogues sound muffled, quick, and current-hidden; monks sound calm and suspended; bards sound elegant and glassy.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.finalVoiceFeel: Graceful, clear, flowing, elegant, refracted like light through water.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Deep-sea peoples, trench lineages, abyssal Dril'thar, deep merfolk, pressure-adapted sea elves, drowned undead, alien aquatic minds, and shadowed waterborn bloodlines use this instead of brighter reef accents. Aquatic races make it almost sung. Dwarven or stone-heavy lineages make it denser and slower. Humans keep it balanced. Aberrant and psionic lineages make pauses longer, stranger, and more hypnotic.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Rangers sound calm and practical; warriors sound clipped and blunt; warlocks sound distant and abyssal; blood hunters sound restrained and haunted; psionic classes sound slow, spaced, and inevitable.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Humans stay neutral and plainspoken. Halflings and smallfolk become friendly and neighborly. Orcs become rougher and more direct. Beastfolk become open, vocal, and physical. Grassland Dril'thar should use Plainwind Common when they belong to rain-fed ponds, seasonal wetlands, prairie pools, or hidden wetland gardens rather than ocean or swamp culture. Grassland birdfolk sound broad, high, and wind-carried.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Merchants sound practical and familiar; rangers sound natural; warriors sound terse; druids sound weather-aware; farmers and scouts sound easygoing but observant.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Dwarves deepen the accent if present in prairie fortresses or railholds. Elves soften it but keep the width and distance. Beastfolk make it expressive and strong. Hobgoblins, military humans, gnolls, giantkin, and strategy-heavy psionic peoples make it stern, organized, and hard-edged. Prairie birdfolk sound sharp, sky-wide, and watchful.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Warriors sound stern; scholars sound formal and heavy; bards sound dramatic; fighters and commanders sound order-heavy; monks and psions sound disciplined.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.finalVoiceFeel: Strong, wide, grounded, hardy, martial when needed.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Halflings make it especially warm. Dwarves make it earthier. Elves make it lighter and lilting. Plantfolk sound seasonal, patient, and root-wise. Domestic beastfolk and hearthbound experimental races become friendly, food-centered, and communal. Rural undead may soften into old-family melancholy rather than horror.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Farmers and artisans sound practical; clerics sound gentle; bards sound cheerful; druids sound harvest-wise; paladins sound neighborly but firm.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Dwarves fit this best and sound very natural. Goliaths, giantkin, stone Genasi, mountain orcs, goatfolk, raptorfolk, avariel, and alpine dragonborn become rugged and breath-heavy. Humans sound cleaner. Orcs sound harsh and mountainous. Avariel and birdfolk make it wind-cut and high-altitude. Snow elves, frost bloodlines, and cold-adapted lineages use Cragthane Burr with frost overlay if no separate arctic biome is available.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Warriors sound rugged; scouts sound clipped; scholars sound surprisingly sharp; barbarians sound storm-heavy; clerics sound ancestral; artificers sound stone-and-forge practical.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Elves make it smooth and lyrical. Halflings make it warm. Beastfolk make it expressive and lively. Satyrs, fey-touched peoples, flowerfolk, vinebound plantfolk, and charismatic miscellaneous lineages flourish here. Celestial or beautiful planar bloodlines become polished, radiant, and emotionally expressive.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Merchants sound polished; bards sound romantic; clerics sound calm and measured; sorcerers sound passionate; warlocks sound seductive or uncanny depending patron.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.finalVoiceFeel: Flowing, fertile, elegant, soft, emotionally expressive.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Dwarves and Duergar make it especially resonant. Humans sound clear. Orcs, dragonborn, reptilian peoples, and deep beastfolk make it heavier and more guttural. Drow, deep gnomes, cave Dril'thar, subterranean amphibians, undead, umbral humans, shadow goblins, constructs, and aberrant lineages use Stonehollow Echo when their home is beneath the surface. Warforged sound metallic and measured; psionic cavern peoples sound echo-spaced and exact.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Mages sound ancient and formal; warriors sound short and echoing; rogues sound low and secretive; artificers sound mechanical and vault-like; clerics sound ancestral or funeral-deep.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.finalVoiceFeel: Echoing, ancient, carved-from-stone, heavy with memory.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Wood Elves use Rootmere Cant rather than Highbranch Lilt when grounded in forest floor culture. Elves soften the hardness. Beastfolk make it strong and vocal. Humans keep it practical. Firbolgs, tabaxi, wolffolk, bearfolk, forest goblins, shadow elves, plantfolk, fungal lineages, forest Dril'thar, and umbral wilderness peoples all fit here. Drow only use this if they are deep-forest shadow operatives rather than cavern-born.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Rangers sound disciplined; scholars sound precise; warriors sound clipped and firm; monks sound controlled and low; druids sound rooted; rogues sound hidden and tense.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.finalVoiceFeel: Dense, structured, rooted, authoritative, old-growth serious.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Humans sound closest to base. Halflings make it warmer. Elves make it smoother. Mixed woodland-and-town lineages, road fey, edge-forest beastfolk, smallfolk traders, birdfolk scouts, and light-footed goblins use this when they belong between forest and settlement rather than deep wilderness. Plantfolk become more social and sunlit.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Merchants sound lively; bards sound expressive; scouts sound relaxed; rogues sound nimble; rangers sound practical but friendly.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: High Elves, treetop elves, fey-touched scholars, archfey bloodlines, birdfolk, gliding beastfolk, arboreal plantfolk, and elegant miscellaneous peoples use this. Elves make it especially elegant. Humans sound refined. Halflings make it playful if they live in tree-villages. Sea Elves do not default here unless raised in aerial or treetop culture; they use reef, deepcurrent, or shore accents instead. Shadow-treetop peoples make it whisper-light and eerie.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Bards sound graceful; scholars sound polished; rogues sound light and quick; warlocks sound lyrical and bargain-coded; monks sound airy and controlled.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Halflings make it friendly. Beastfolk make it rhythmic. Orcs make it rough and muddy. Swamp and marsh Dril'thar use Mirecurl Drawl, especially lineages tied to spawning pools, rot, renewal, gravewater, and wetland prophecy. Dhampirs, drowned undead, swamp hags, reptilian lineages, ooze-touched peoples, fungal plantfolk, and shadowed marshfolk all fit here.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Rangers sound local and practical; clerics sound slow and steady; rogues sound secretive; blood hunters sound haunted and restrained; druids sound rot-and-renewal wise.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.finalVoiceFeel: Slow, earthy, wet, lived-in, haunted when death-touched.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Humans sound natural. Elves sound breezy. Aquatic races make it very soft and fluid. Shoreline Dril'thar, coastal halflings, beach beastfolk, tide-touched Genasi, reptilian sunning cultures, and water-grass nomads use this when they are coastal but not reef-centered. Sea Elves raised in coastal trade towns may use Brightshore Flow rather than Reefglass Lilt.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Merchants sound relaxed; bards sound sunlit and melodic; warriors sound brisk; clerics sound open and blessing-like; rangers sound shore-practical.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Aquatic races make it strongest. Halflings make it welcoming. Dwarves make it firmer and more grounded if they are reef-builders or harbor-smiths. Dragonborn and reptilian lineages add command and throat-weight. Sea Elves raised around reef communities use Wavebloom Welcome for above-water reef culture and Reefglass Lilt for fully underwater reef culture. Reef Dril'thar, coralborn, turtlefolk, reef Genasi, and tropical birdfolk fit strongly here.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Clerics sound peaceful; bards sound musical; scouts sound light-footed; paladins sound ceremonial and community-protective; druids sound reef-wise.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Elves smooth it out. Dwarves make it rustic. Beastfolk make it earthy and lively. Kender, hedgefolk, jerbeen, ferretfolk tinkerers, satyrs, forest-floor fey, burrow smallfolk, woodland beastfolk, and bramble plantfolk use this when they live between canopy and ground. Wood Elves may use Bramblewood Burr if they are village-woodland rather than deep-forest traditionalists.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Rangers sound very natural; artisans sound local; warriors sound sturdy; bards sound folk-tale bright; rogues sound playful and quick.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.finalVoiceFeel: Rustic, layered, grounded, woodland, friendly but sturdy.

biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.raceLineageBloodlineOverlayBehavior: Humans sound plain and practical. Halflings make it friendly. Orcs make it blunt and workmanlike. Gnomes, Geisamahi, autognomes, constructed farmhands, mixed villages, beastfolk laborers, plantfolk cultivators, and experimental civic lineages fit well here. This is the best fallback for practical multi-ancestry settlements that are neither fully rural-hearth nor fully wild.
biomeAccentCarriersPreserved.classOverlayBehavior: Merchants sound efficient; farmers sound matter-of-fact; scholars sound neat and organized; artificers sound workshop-practical; clerics sound community-service focused.

recommendedBiomeAccentCarriersForCategor.finalVoiceFeel: Open, rolling, maritime, lively, salt-air social.
Graceful, clear, flowing, elegant, refracted like light through water.
Flowing, fertile, elegant, soft, emotionally expressive.
Echoing, ancient, carved-from-stone, heavy with memory.
Dense, structured, rooted, authoritative, old-growth serious.
Slow, earthy, wet, lived-in, haunted when death-touched.

lineageDivergenceRules.title: Biome beats ancestry when the body or culture depends on environment.
lineageDivergenceRules.rule: A Sea Elf, Wood Elf, Drow, Avariel, and High Elf should not all share one "Elf accent." Their accent follows lived biome first, then Elven overlay second.

lineageDivergenceRules.title: Bloodline beats broad category when the bloodline changes habitat.
lineageDivergenceRules.rule: A Fire Genasi in an ocean-surface rebel port may use Tidecrest Cant with ember pressure, while an Earth Genasi in a mountain hold may use Cragthane Burr or Stonehollow Echo.

lineageDivergenceRules.title: Culture beats biology when raised outside ancestral terrain.
lineageDivergenceRules.rule: A reefborn Dragonborn raised in a floating settlement may use Tidecrest Cant instead of Wavebloom Welcome. A Wood Elf raised in treetop academies may use Highbranch Lilt instead of Rootmere Cant.

lineageDivergenceRules.title: Aquatic peoples split heavily by water type.
lineageDivergenceRules.rule: Reef communities use Reefglass Lilt underwater or Wavebloom Welcome above-water. Deep ocean communities use Deepcurrent Song. Floating harbors use Tidecrest Cant. Coastal grass-and-water cultures use Brightshore Flow.

lineageDivergenceRules.title: Dril'thar must diverge by water ecology.
lineageDivergenceRules.rule: Grassland Dril'thar = Plainwind Common with rain-fed wetland softness. Swamp Dril'thar = Mirecurl Drawl with gravewater/rot/renewal rhythm. Reef Dril'thar = Reefglass Lilt underwater or Wavebloom Welcome above-water. Ocean Dril'thar = Tidecrest Cant for floating/ocean settlements or Deepcurrent Song for deep-water lineages. Forest/Rainforest Dril'thar = Rootmere Cant, Sundapple Tongue, or Bramblewood Burr depending canopy depth and settlement style.

lineageDivergenceRules.title: Shadow lineages follow the shadowed version of the biome.
lineageDivergenceRules.rule: Umbral Human in a city-grassland region may use Plainwind Common darkened by shadow. Shadow Goblin in cavern systems uses Stonehollow Echo. Shadar-Kai in forest gloom uses Rootmere Cant.

lineageDivergenceRules.title: Constructed beings follow maker culture plus operating environment.
lineageDivergenceRules.rule: Warforged forged in Deep Cavern use Stonehollow Echo. Autognomes and workshop constructs in mixed farm/forest towns use Millfield Practical. Ship constructs use Tidecrest Cant.

lineageDivergenceRules.title: Planar bloodlines should not all sound celestial or fiendish by default.
lineageDivergenceRules.rule: A celestial mountain guardian may use Cragthane Burr with radiant clarity; a fiendish swamp exile may use Mirecurl Drawl with infernal heat; an astral scholar may use Highbranch Lilt, Deepcurrent Song, or Stonehollow Echo depending home culture.

lineageDivergenceRules.title: Birdfolk split by flight environment.
lineageDivergenceRules.rule: Avariel and cliff raptors use Cragthane Burr; canopy birdfolk use Highbranch Lilt; shorebirds use Brightshore Flow or Wavebloom Welcome; prairie hawkfolk use Ironstep Cant or Plainwind Common.

lineageDivergenceRules.title: Plantfolk split by growth pattern.
lineageDivergenceRules.rule: Crop and orchard plantfolk use Hearthfield Brogue or Millfield Practical; deep-root forest plantfolk use Rootmere Cant; vine and flower valley plantfolk use Vinesong Flow; fungal/swamp plantfolk use Mirecurl Drawl.

lineageDivergenceRules.title: Beastfolk and experimental races split by animal ecology.
lineageDivergenceRules.rule: Catfolk in deep forest use Rootmere Cant; ferretfolk tinkerers in mixed settlements use Millfield Practical or Bramblewood Burr; aquatic hybrids use water accents; hoofed grassland beastfolk use Plainwind Common or Ironstep Cant.

quickBiomeAccentExamples.betterBiomeSpecificChoice: Deep Forest Wood Elf = Rootmere Cant; woodland village Wood Elf = Bramblewood Burr; treetop noble Wood Elf = Highbranch Lilt.
Reef Sea Elf underwater = Reefglass Lilt; deep Sea Elf = Deepcurrent Song; coastal Sea Elf trader = Brightshore Flow; above-reef Sea Elf = Wavebloom Welcome.
Underdark Drow = Stonehollow Echo; deep-forest shadow operative Drow = Rootmere Cant; swamp exile Drow = Mirecurl Drawl.
Grassland Dril'thar = Plainwind Common; swamp Dril'thar = Mirecurl Drawl; reef Dril'thar = Reefglass Lilt or Wavebloom Welcome; ocean Dril'thar = Tidecrest Cant or Deepcurrent Song.
Reef Dragonborn = Wavebloom Welcome with draconic command; cavern Dragonborn = Stonehollow Echo with heavier throat-weight; mountain Dragonborn = Cragthane Burr with thunder-weight.
Deep forge Warforged = Stonehollow Echo; village Autognome = Millfield Practical; shipboard construct = Tidecrest Cant.
Ocean-surface Fire Genasi = Tidecrest Cant with ember pressure; valley Fire Genasi = Vinesong Flow with dramatic warmth; mountain Fire Genasi = Cragthane Burr with volcanic weight.
Forest Tabaxi = Rootmere Cant; valley Satyr = Vinesong Flow; prairie Gnoll = Ironstep Cant; coastal otterfolk = Brightshore Flow.

categoryDeepVoiceProfile.dominionInfluence: Mistress of crossroads, curses, planar mysteries, and supernatural bloodlines.
categoryDeepVoiceProfile.categorySpeechSummary: Otherworldly resonance; celestial lines brighten, fiendish lines harden, cursed lines roughen.
categoryDeepVoiceProfile.mathOverwriteNote: Legacy speechParameters were converted into the deep engine vector below. Runtime should use deepCategoryMath and deepRaceMath, not older shallow pace/formant-only fields.

speciesTextureRole.sourceFilterRole: modifies glottal source noise, formants, resonance, mouth shape, articulation, timing, and special texture layers
speciesTextureRole.vocoderRole: conditions speaker/style vector and artifact QA expectations
speciesTextureRole.browserTtsRole: collapses to pitch/rate/voice-selection score while preserving text markers, pauses, and SSML-lite hints

races.name: Aasimar
races.descriptionSourceSummary: Aasimar are celestial-touched beings infused with divine energy and purpose. They often feel a guiding moral force or destiny influencing their lives.

textAndProsodyHooks.hesitationInsertion: Use stutter slider and race tension/secrecy values to choose repetition, filler, or silence; do not force disability-coded speech unless explicitly selected.
textAndProsodyHooks.accentPersistence: Biome accent remains selected even when race influence fades; race influence changes body/texture, not the cultural accent carrier.
textAndProsodyHooks.textureLayerMix: specialTextureMix fields add fantasy-only layers such as draconic growl, avian lift, metallic resonance, undead whisper, psionic phase, click/croak, or plant rustle.

races.name: Tiefling

selectionOrder: apply lineage route if a lineage/bloodline/ecology route is available
apply gender identity from gender_identities file
